stories.lead: Snakes taste the air with their forked tongues. They flick it in and out, collecting scent particles because that's why their tongues are forked—two points to triangulate smells. Watch this one twice: the split tip lets them smell left, right, and everywhere in between. Ever wonder why their mouths stay shut while they do it? Me too.

stories.replay_prompt: End by pointing back to the tongue smell, so viewers rewatch the snakes clip.
stories.script: Snakes taste the air with their forked tongues. They flick it in and out, collecting scent particles because that's why their tongues are forked—two points to triangulate smells. Watch this one twice: the split tip lets them smell left, right, and everywhere in between. Ever wonder why their mouths stay shut while they do it? Me too.
